title: Will-o'-the-wisp first anomaly

target.label: Marsh light
target.description: A reported floating light with witness testimony and weak optical diagnostics.
limitations: Witness reports are not physical diagnostics.
No direct particle, neutron, gamma, or internal measurement exists.

questions.text: What energy source is consistent with the supplied observations?
Is there evidence for an ionized plasma?
Is the fusion claim supported or contradicted?

interpretationRisks.description: A visible glow can be mistaken for plasma or fusion before calibrated diagnostics are available.
interpretationRisks.mitigation: Keep plasma and fusion as claims to test, and require mediated readouts beyond human vision.
limitations: The risk remains open because no particle-product diagnostic is present.

interpretationRisks.description: Brightness modulation can combine source behavior, motion, background, and instrument response.
interpretationRisks.mitigation: Treat the FFT peak as a modulation readout only, not as an energy-source identity.
limitations: The fixture has no independent motion or background channel.

artifacts.label: Human visual report

calibration.description: The observer's visual response is not instrument-calibrated.
limitations: Human vision cannot separate plasma emission, thermal glow, and fusion-product signatures unaided.

contributions.description: The visible glow may include plasma emission.
limitations: A visual report alone cannot identify the emitting mechanism.

contributions.description: The visible glow may be chemical luminescence.
limitations: No calibrated spectrum is available in the testimony.

artifacts.description: Witness report of a floating visible light.
limitations: This is a subjective visual observation, not a calibrated optical diagnostic.

source.label: Human visual report source record
limitations: Static investigation fixture metadata; not live telemetry.

artifacts.label: Emission intensity

calibration.description: Camera response is estimated from field notes rather than a lab calibration.
limitations: Brightness changes are comparable only within this trace.

contributions.description: Ionized emission is a candidate contributor to the brightness trace.
limitations: The trace has no line-resolved spectral evidence.

contributions.description: Thermal glow can modulate visible brightness.
limitations: The camera is not a bolometer.

contributions.description: Sensor noise and compression may create or smear small peaks.
limitations: No dark-frame correction is supplied.

bands.label: Visible brightness flicker
bands.description: Temporal brightness variation of the visible light.
limitations: A flicker frequency is not an energy-source identity.
The peak may be source modulation, motion, aliasing, or instrument response.

frequencyAnalyses.description: Fourier decomposition of the visible brightness trace.
assumptions: The field sample rate is approximately stable.
limitations: FFT peaks constrain modulation; they do not prove plasma or fusion.
Brightness alone cannot identify fusion.

source.label: Emission intensity source record
limitations: Static investigation fixture metadata; not live telemetry.

artifacts.label: Visible spectrum

calibration.description: A coarse wavelength response correction has been applied.
limitations: The correction is insufficient for fusion-product discrimination.

contributions.description: Line-like visible features could be plasma emission.
limitations: Visible lines alone do not imply fusion.

contributions.description: Ambient sky and reflected light contribute to the spectrum.
limitations: The background model is field-derived.

bands.label: Visible optical carrier band
bands.description: Carrier-frequency range for the visible spectrum.
limitations: Visible carrier frequencies do not by themselves identify the source.
The feature is not reaction-product evidence.

frequencyAnalyses.description: Frequency-domain representation of the visible electromagnetic carrier.
assumptions: Wavelength-to-frequency conversion uses vacuum light speed approximation.
limitations: The carrier spectrum must be combined with particle or high-energy diagnostics for fusion claims.

artifacts.description: Coarse visible spectrum captured near the reported light.
limitations: No neutron, gamma, or particle diagnostics are supplied.

source.label: Visible spectrum source record
limitations: Static investigation fixture metadata; not live telemetry.
The spectrum is too coarse to infer ion temperature.

assumptions: A glowing atmospheric event is not automatically plasma or fusion.
limitations: Fusion is treated as a claim to test, not as a premise.

claims.statement: The supplied will-o'-the-wisp evidence does not support a fusion claim.
assumptions: The supplied artifact set is complete for this mission step.
limitations: Absence of evidence is not proof that no fusion source exists.
Educational investigation package only.
No real hardware control path.
This is a subjective visual observation, not a calibrated optical diagnostic.

assumptions: The field sample rate is approximately stable.
limitations: Brightness alone cannot identify fusion.

assumptions: Wavelength-to-frequency conversion uses vacuum light speed approximation.
limitations: No neutron, gamma, or particle diagnostics are supplied.
